title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 536.019
text:
*536.019.  Effective date of rules — contingent effective date. — 1.  Notwithstanding other provisions of this chapter to the contrary, a final order of rulemaking shall not take effect prior to the expiration of thirty legislative days of a regular session after such order of rulemaking has been filed with the general assembly by providing a copy thereof to the joint committee on administrative rules and the secretary of state.
  2.  This section shall become effective only upon the expiration of twenty calendar days following the:
  (1)  Failure of the executive to sign executive order number 97-97; or
  (2)  Modification, amendment or rescission of executive order number 97-97; or
  (3)  An agency's failure to hold the rule in abeyance as required by executive order number 97-97; or
  (4)  Declaration by a court with jurisdiction that section 536.024 or any portion of executive order number 97-97 is unconstitutional or invalid for any reason.  ­­­
(L. 1997 H.B. 850) 
*Contingent effective date, see subsection 2 of this section. 
*Revisor's note:  To date, the contingencies contained in this section regarding Executive Order 97-97 have not occurred.
